A British Traveller's Guide

DiscoverPoland

Six extraordinary cities. One unforgettable journey.

~2hfrom London
6unmissable cities
17UNESCO sites
£5avg. meal cost
Explore

"Poland is one of Europe's most rewarding yet underrated destinations — a land of magnificent medieval cities, dramatic mountain landscapes, amber Baltic coasts and extraordinary cuisine. Just 2 hours from London, with incredible value and genuine warmth."

01

Cities to Explore

Click any city to see detailed attractions & tips

✦ Culture
01

Kraków

"The Royal Capital"

Poland's most beloved city. Wawel Royal Castle towers above the Vistula, while Europe's largest medieval market square and the vibrant Kazimierz quarter await.

MedievalRoyalJewish heritageUNESCO
See attractions & tips
⚓ Baltic Coast
02

Gdańsk

"Pearl of the Baltic"

A Hanseatic treasure on the Baltic Sea with soaring Gothic architecture and colourful gabled merchant houses. Birthplace of the Solidarity movement that changed Europe.

BalticHanseaticAmberHistory
See attractions & tips
🌈 Vibrant
03

Wrocław

"City of a Hundred Bridges"

Built on twelve islands, Wrocław enchants with an explosion of colour. The Market Square is flanked by pastel tenements of cobalt, tangerine and rose. Spot 300+ hidden bronze dwarfs!

ColourfulIslandsBronze dwarfsUNESCO
See attractions & tips
⛰️ Mountains
04

Zakopane

"Winter Capital of Poland"

Nestled beneath dramatic Tatra peaks, this mountain resort enchants year-round. Hike to the glacial Morskie Oko lake or take the cable car up Kasprowy Wierch for views into Slovakia.

MountainsHikingSkiingFolk art
See attractions & tips
🐐 History
05

Poznań

"Birthplace of Poland"

Poland's oldest city. Each noon, two mechanical goats emerge from the Town Hall clock to butt heads. Sample the famous Saint Martin croissants and visit the original Polish Cathedral.

MedievalRenaissanceRogalCommerce
See attractions & tips
🏙️ Capital
06

Warsaw

"The Phoenix City"

Poland's dynamic capital, rebuilt from rubble after WWII, blends its UNESCO Old Town with glass skyscrapers. A thriving cultural scene with world-class museums, jazz clubs and galleries.

CapitalUNESCOModernCulture
See attractions & tips

Kraków

Wawel Castle & the Royal Capital

Top Attractions

  • 🏰
    Wawel Royal Castle
    Poland's most important monument. Royal apartments, cathedral and dragon's den. Arrive early — timed tickets required.
  • ⛪
    St Mary's Basilica
    Gothic masterpiece on the Main Square. Every hour a bugle call (hejnał) plays from its tower — a 700-year tradition.
  • 🏛️
    Main Market Square (Rynek)
    Europe's largest medieval square. Don't miss the Cloth Hall (Sukiennice) filled with amber and folk art souvenirs.
  • 🕍
    Kazimierz Jewish Quarter
    Atmospheric streets lined with synagogues, galleries and excellent cafés. Schindler's Factory nearby.
  • ⛏️
    Wieliczka Salt Mine
    UNESCO wonder just 15 mins away — underground chapels and lakes carved entirely from salt.
  • 🕯️
    Auschwitz-Birkenau
    Essential day trip, 1.5 hrs away. Book guided tours well in advance. A profoundly important visit.

🍽️ Must eat: Pierogi at Pierogi Ruskie on Plac Nowy. Stay: Hotel Copernicus or boutique hotels in Kazimierz. Best time: May–June or September for smaller crowds.

Gdańsk

The Hanseatic Pearl of the Baltic

Top Attractions

  • 🚪
    Long Lane (ul. Długa) & Golden Gate
    The grandest street in Poland — flanked by colourful merchant houses. Walk the full length, it's breathtaking.
  • ⚓
    Żuraw Crane Gate
    Medieval Gothic port crane, the largest in medieval Europe. Now a fascinating maritime museum inside.
  • ✊
    European Solidarity Centre
    World-class museum on the Solidarity movement that toppled communism. Emotional and beautifully designed.
  • 🕊️
    Westerplatte
    Where WWII began on 1 September 1939. A powerful monument overlooking the Baltic.
  • 🏖️
    Sopot & the Baltic Beach
    20 mins by train. Europe's longest wooden pier, sandy beach and a lively resort atmosphere.
  • 💎
    Amber Workshops
    Gdańsk is the amber capital of the world. Watch craftsmen work and buy direct from the workshops.

🍽️ Must eat: Fresh herring and Baltic cod at any harbour restaurant. Drink: Goldwasser liqueur — invented here in the 16th century! Best time: July–August for beach weather.

Wrocław

City of a Hundred Bridges

Top Attractions

  • 🌈
    Rynek Market Square
    One of Europe's largest and most colourful squares. The Gothic Town Hall is a masterpiece — climb the tower for views.
  • 🐾
    Wrocław Dwarfs Trail
    Over 300 bronze dwarfs hidden across the city. Pick up a map and spend a delightful afternoon hunting them down.
  • ⛪
    Cathedral Island (Ostrów Tumski)
    The most romantic corner of Wrocław — gas-lit streets, twin-spired cathedral, and the city's oldest churches.
  • 🏛️
    Centennial Hall
    UNESCO-listed modernist masterpiece from 1913. The largest reinforced concrete structure of its era.
  • 🌿
    Wrocław Botanical Garden & ZOO
    The ZOO is one of Europe's finest — over 10,000 animals. The botanical garden is lovely in spring.
  • 🌉
    Tumski Bridge & Love Locks
    Romantic footbridge covered in padlocks. Cross it at dusk when the old town gaslight lamplighter makes his rounds.

🍽️ Must eat: Śledź (herring) and craft beer at any Rynek restaurant. Fun fact: Wrocław was European Capital of Culture 2016. Best time: April–October. The Christmas market is magical!

Zakopane

Winter Capital & Tatra Gateway

Top Attractions

  • 🏔️
    Morskie Oko Lake
    Poland's most beautiful lake, nestled in a glacial cirque. 9km hike through Tatra forest — absolutely breathtaking. Bring sturdy shoes.
  • 🚡
    Cable Car to Kasprowy Wierch
    Rises to 1,959m in minutes. On clear days you can see both Poland and Slovakia. Book online in advance in summer.
  • 🛍️
    Krupówki Promenade
    The lively pedestrian street with folk craft stalls, smoked oscypek cheese vendors and traditional górale (highlander) musicians.
  • 🌄
    Gubałówka Hill
    A gentle funicular ride to panoramic views over Zakopane and the Tatras. Perfect sunset spot, great for families.
  • ⛷️
    Tatra National Park
    100+ hiking trails in summer, ski slopes in winter. The park covers 212 km² of stunning alpine wilderness.
  • 🏠
    Villa Koliba & Folk Architecture
    The first villa in the Zakopane Style — a uniquely Polish fusion of folk and Art Nouveau. Now a museum.

🧀 Must eat: Grilled oscypek with cranberry jam, and bigos stew in a highland inn (karczma). Best time: July–August for hiking, December–March for skiing. Note: Mountain weather changes fast — always pack a rain layer!

Poznań

Birthplace of Poland & the Goats' City

Top Attractions

  • 🐐
    Town Hall & the Mechanical Goats
    Every day at noon, two mechanical goats emerge from the Renaissance Town Hall and butt heads exactly 12 times. A must-see!
  • 🏛️
    Old Market Square (Stary Rynek)
    One of the most beautiful squares in Poland — colourful Renaissance merchant houses surrounding the Gothic Town Hall.
  • ⛪
    Ostrów Tumski — Cathedral Island
    The oldest part of Poland, where Christianity first took root in 966 AD. The twin-towered cathedral holds the tombs of Poland's first kings.
  • 🏰
    Imperial Castle (Zamek Cesarski)
    Built for Kaiser Wilhelm II — the last royal castle built in Europe. Now a cultural centre with excellent exhibitions.
  • 🥐
    Rogal Croissant Trail
    The Saint Martin's croissant is a Poznań institution — flaky, filled with poppy paste. Try them at Cukiernia Kandulski.
  • 🏊
    Malta Lake & Park
    Artificial lake with rowing, cycling, a mini-railway and the New Zoo. Perfect for a relaxed half day.

🥐 Must eat: Rogal świętomarciński (the famous croissant) and żurek with eggs. Best time: June for the Malta International Theatre Festival. Tip: Poznań is only 2.5 hrs from Warsaw and 3 hrs from Wrocław by fast train — perfect combo!

Warsaw

The Phoenix Capital of Poland

Top Attractions

  • 🏙️
    Old Town (Stare Miasto) — UNESCO
    Entirely rebuilt after WWII from paintings and photos. A miraculous achievement. The Royal Castle and colourful Kanonia Square are highlights.
  • ✊
    Warsaw Uprising Museum
    Poland's most visited museum — tells the story of the 1944 uprising against Nazi occupation. Deeply moving and brilliantly designed. Allow 3 hours.
  • 🌳
    Łazienki Palace & Park
    The Palace on the Water — a stunning baroque island palace in a vast royal park. Free Chopin concerts every Sunday in summer.
  • 🎹
    Chopin Museum & Birthplace
    Interactive museum dedicated to Poland's greatest composer. Touch-screen music exploration — exceptional for music lovers.
  • 🏛️
    POLIN Museum of Polish Jews
    World-class museum on 1000 years of Jewish life in Poland. One of the best museums in Europe — not to be missed.
  • 🎭
    Praga District
    Warsaw's bohemian East Bank — artists' studios, vintage shops, craft bars and street art. The city's coolest neighbourhood.

🍽️ Must eat: Zapiekanka (Polish baguette) at Praga market. Drink: Craft beer at Craft Beer Muranów. Best time: May–September. Tip: The metro is fast and easy — buy a day ticket and explore freely.

"Where history whispers from every cobblestone"

02

When to Visit

Every season brings a different magic to Poland

🌸
Spring
April – May
★★★★☆

Blooming parks, Easter traditions, mild temps. Perfect city walks without summer crowds.

☀️
Summer
June – August
★★★★★

Warm and lively. Best for Tatra hiking, Baltic beaches and rooftop bars.

🍂
Autumn
Sep – October
★★★★☆

Golden forests, fewer tourists, mushroom-picking season and crisp clear air.

❄️
Winter
Dec – February
★★★☆☆

Magical Christmas markets in Kraków & Wrocław. Skiing in Zakopane.

03

Must-Try Polish Food

Hearty, honest and utterly delicious

🥟
Pierogi

Poland's beloved stuffed dumplings — potato & cheese, meat or sauerkraut. Order fried for extra indulgence.

🥣
Żurek

Sour rye soup with sausage and hard-boiled egg, often served in a hollowed bread bowl.

🥩
Bigos

Hunter's stew of sauerkraut, cabbage and smoked meats simmered for hours. Poland's soul food.

🧀
Oscypek

Smoked sheep's cheese from the Tatras, grilled and served warm with cranberry jam.

🥐
Rogal Świętomarciński

Poznań's flaky croissant filled with white poppy seed paste. Sold by the kilo.

🍰
Sernik

Polish cheesecake made with twaróg curd cheese — denser, richer and creamier than any other.

🍺
Polish Beer

Żywiec, Tyskie & Okocim — excellent lagers found everywhere, incredibly affordable.

🥃
Żubrówka Vodka

Bison grass vodka — mix with apple juice for the classic "Tatanka" cocktail.

04

Essential Phrases

A little Polish goes a long way — locals love it!

Hello
Cześć
cheshch
Thank you
Dziękuję
jen-koo-yeh
Please / You're welcome
Proszę
pro-sheh
Excuse me / Sorry
Przepraszam
pshe-pra-sham
Good morning
Dzień dobry
jen dob-ree
Cheers!
Na zdrowie!
nah zdro-vyeh
How much is this?
Ile to kosztuje?
ee-leh to kosh-too-yeh
Delicious!
Pyszne!
pish-neh
05

Practical Tips

Everything a British traveller needs to know

💷
Currency

Polish Złoty (PLN). Very affordable — meals from £5, pints from £1.50. Cards widely accepted everywhere.

✈️
Flights

Direct from London, Manchester & Edinburgh with LOT, Ryanair & Wizz Air. Around 2–2.5 hours flying time.

🚄
Getting Around

PKP Intercity trains connect all cities quickly and cheaply. Book at intercity.pl. City trams are excellent.

🗣️
Language

English widely spoken in hotels and restaurants. Younger Poles are very comfortable in English.

🛡️
Safety

One of Europe's safest countries. Standard precautions apply. GHIC cards accepted for healthcare.

🌡️
Weather

Summers 20–30°C, winters cold & snowy (great for Zakopane). Pack layers for spring/autumn.

🔌
Plugs

Type C/E sockets, 230V. Bring a standard European travel adaptor — cheap and available everywhere.

📱
Mobile & Data

UK SIMs work in Poland. Check roaming policy. Local SIM cards very cheap at newsagents.

🇵🇱

Witaj w Polsce — Welcome to Poland

A country of extraordinary history, warm hospitality and unforgettable flavours

Share this guide · Enjoy your journey · Smacznego!
